questions.prompt: Which of these settings can a Provisioning Template carry? (Select all that apply.)
choices: The list of extensions to create
Music on Hold (with its audio file)
Allowed country codes for outbound dialling
questions.explanation: Templates carry baseline settings (allowed IPs, music on hold, distinctive caller ID, country codes, contact visibility, device naming, speech-to-text). They do not carry per-customer data like extensions, queues, IVRs, time conditions, recording rules, trunks, or DIDs. Those are built per-customer in PSE after the template applies.

questions.prompt: If you edit a Provisioning Template after applying it to a PBX, the changes flow back into that PBX automatically.
questions.explanation: Template application is a one-shot push at apply time. Later edits to the template do not retroactively reach PBXes that already received it. This is by design: customers override settings post-apply, and a retroactive push would clobber their overrides.
